title: `podman machine` command error on Windows over SSH
body:
### Issue Description

I've connected to a Windows 11 box using [its built-in OpenSSH server.](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/administration/openssh/openssh_install_firstuse?tabs=gui&pivots=windows-server-2022). Over this SSH connection, it is not possible to run `podman machine` commands:

```bash
$ podman machine ssh
Error: failed to start command C:\Users\sinay\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps\wsl.exe [-l --quiet --running]: fork/exec C:\Users\sinay\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps\wsl.exe: The file cannot be accessed by the system.
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Set up a Windows 11 machine with OpenSSH server enabled
2. Install Podman and initialize its WSL2 VM
3. Verify that `podman machine info` in a graphical session works
4. Verify that `podman machine info` in an SSH session does not work

### podman info output

```yaml
Podman v5.3.1
Windows 11 on x64
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

comments.body: are there assumptions about terminal types on windows or perhaps access rights when remote?  like, do normal wsl commands work over ssh ?

<s>Did a bit of digging. I think I found the root cause:

https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/7900
https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/9197
https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/9231

Although I'm not entirely sure because these issues claim that it was fixed in some `2.0.0-dev` version, but in my case the installed WSL is well past that version (`2.3.26.0`).</s>

Correction: running `wsl --distribution podman-machine-default` directly over SSH does actually work. This makes me think it's a podman issue.

Ah okay. Found something interesting:

```bash
$ where wsl
C:\Windows\System32\wsl.exe
C:\Users\sinay\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps\wsl.exe
```

So it seems like there are two `wsl` executables for some reason. When I run `wsl --distribution podman-machine-default`, it runs the first one without problems. But `podman machine` commands delegate to the second one, which does not run.

To confirm this, I explicitly-pathed to the second executable and observed similar:

```bash
# Using Bash but it doesn't seem to matter; Powershell has similar behaviour
$ /c/Users/sinay/AppData/Local/Microsoft/WindowsApps/wsl --distribution podman-machine-default
bash: /c/Users/sinay/AppData/Local/Microsoft/WindowsApps/wsl: Permission denied
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Bump. I'm facing this issue with a Windows Server 2022 VM and Ubuntu WSL. I've set the default shell as PowerShell 7 and connect to it via SSH from my local laptop. I get the same error when running `wsl` command. 

From GUI (using Remote Desktop), I can login to the VM and access `wsl` from PowerShell properly.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3b14531b-704d-4f1f-a1e5-b7afed78e1f3)

**Edit:**  Actually it works when running the `wsl.exe` present in `C:\Program Files\WSL\wsl.exe`. But this is not reported by `where wsl` strangely.
https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/7900 is supposedly resolved 2 weeks ago, but i'm still seeing this issue with podman when using the latest WSL release.

Found out today that `podman compose` (which uses to `docker-compose` as its compose plugin) has the same problem.

Also, found a semi-solution. I simply deleted `/c/Users/<USERNAME>/AppData/Local/Microsoft/WindowsApps/wsl.exe` (or rename it to `wsl.exe.bak` or something), this way `wsl` resolves to `/c/Windows/System32/wsl.exe` and everything works.

Not sure if this trick will have any adverse repercussions, but at least this solves this issue.
